attacking_style: Pure sprinter who relies on final 200-300m positioning and tactical awareness rather than raw lead-out power. Shows ability to improvise when boxed in late sprint situations.
team_tactics: Bahrain lead-out frequently fails or finishes early, forcing him to sprint solo from 300m+ out. Team depth insufficient for consistent sprint trains in WorldTour stage races.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages where positioning intelligence can compensate for weak lead-out support. Struggles on any stage with categorized climbs, getting distanced on final ascents.
vulnerability: Completely exposed on any hilly terrain—distanced on final climbs multiple times. Deteriorates physically in multi-day tours (Dauphine withdrawal). Technical finales show inconsistency (chicane error 2023).
key_patterns: Repeatedly isolated in final kilometer after team support exhausted (4 observations)
Converts poor positioning to podium results through tactical improvisation, not power (3 instances)
Cannot survive even moderate climbing stages in stage races (2 confirmed DNF/distanced)
Year-over-year tactical regression evident: 2023 chicane error on same Tirreno stage he won 2022
Consistently finishes second when lead-out fails, suggesting threshold just below top-tier sprinters
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Tirreno shows tactical regression (chicane mistake on previously won stage), pattern of physical struggles in stage races continues
